content.text: The squad's ride is infrastructure. Infrastructure has a mechanic.

The Rigger owns vehicles and transport: upkeep, modding, and the convoy economy. The trade bleeds from Tech — anyone can drive a truck on a Tech check; the Rigger has the truck, and keeps it running when the Char fatigues the frame and the Bloom grows into the engine.

content.title: Rigger
content.subtitle: The wheels

entries.label: Field verb
entries.value: clearing a vehicle drain on mission (+1 PP, max 1/mission)

content.desc: PP economy, tiers, and the One Name rule live on Professions Overview. Vehicle rules — frames, Seal, Capacity, drains, chases — live on Vehicles.

content.text: The Garage Habit (entry, 4 PP): opens the trade — the Work verb and the field verb are yours.
Grease Sense (T2, 4 PP): any vehicle's condition, mods, and history on sight.
Route Rat (T2, 5 PP): your convoy legs take −1 frame fatigue (the Char fatigues frames — see Vehicles).
Chop Access (T2, 6 PP): vehicle mods install at −25%. Obligation: chop shops remember faces.
Hard Brake Doctrine (T2, 5 PP): advantage on chase Checks you initiate (chases are contested driver Checks over band separation — see Vehicles).
Smuggler's Frame (T3, 7 PP): your hidden compartments beat Standard inspections outright; Hard inspections contest.
Fleet Hands (T3, 6 PP): two squad vehicles, one upkeep.
Rigger's Name (T3, 8 PP): corp logistics subcontracts you — Name rates, and the gates know your plates. Both edges.

content.label: DESIGN GAP
content.text: The vehicle customization catalog (compartments, freezers, fuel, sealing) is a parked module. Chop Access prices installs against it and Smuggler's Frame assumes hidden compartments exist, but the catalog itself is not yet written — parked, not designed.
Vehicles are Shells you sit inside — Value, Absorb, Frame HP, Seal, Capacity, drains, and chases all live on Vehicles. The Rigger's upkeep clears the drains.
The convoy economy: the Char's 5:1 compression — convoys, gates, tariffs, smuggling — lives on Vehicles. The Guide knows the way; the Rigger keeps the wheels under it (see Guide).
The cargo chain: the Carver's lots ride in your Capacity — carving creates cargo, cargo needs the van, the van needs the Rigger (see Carver).
Vehicle registration confirmed. Your plates are now recognized at all participating checkpoints. Recognition is not a service that can be discontinued.
